summary: Northern and southern homo offers raised again; BSR T3034 to 31,000+
Other PP lines flat with no further declines; sentiment improving
CNPC/Jinneng yarn still quoted 1,260-1,265 USD/ton CIF, seen too high
paras: In the PP homo line today (15/7), many domestic sellers continued to adjust their offers further upward. For instance, in the north, most sellers raised the offer for NSRP's PP 114G to 31,000 dong/kg incl. VAT, with some sellers offering up to 31,500 dong/kg incl. VAT. “The market has almost no more sellers offering PP yarn below 31,000 dong/kg incl. VAT,” a northern source shared.

Similarly, in the south, Hyosung Vina's PP yarn offer and BSR's PP T3034 selling price today have also been proposed higher, to 31,000-31,500 dong/kg incl. VAT... Thus, in just the first 3 days of the week, the domestic PP yarn price level has risen about 1,500-2,000 dong/kg versus the end of last week.

Notably, according to feedback from some market participants, some customers who signed for forward-delivery purchases back in late June/early July are now facing the risk of not receiving their goods on time.

Apart from the PP homo line, offers for other PP items today were mostly flat, with no further declines. The reason PP copo, Film, and coating offers have not yet risen in line with the general trend of PP homo this round is mainly that price levels remain high.

Accordingly, for PP copo, in the north, many trading sellers offered S-Oil Korea-origin PP copo or Chinese-origin PP copo at 34,000-35,000 dong/kg incl. VAT; in the south, the common transaction level is 33,000-34,000 dong/kg incl. VAT. For PP Film, in the south, offers range from 35,000-37,000 dong/kg incl. VAT, about 1,000 dong/kg lower than offers from northern trading companies. As for PP coating, offers range from 39,000-41,000 dong/kg incl. VAT, depending on origin...

A positive signal is that, with the resin price increases over the past 3 days, trading sentiment has become more active and buyers are replenishing more, which is helping sellers clear inventory faster and hold higher selling prices rather than being pressured into deep discounts as before.

On imports, this week the distributor for IRPC of Thailand offered PP for a shipment delivering in late August/early September 2026 to the Vietnamese market. Accordingly, IRPC Thailand's PP offers this month mostly fell sharply by 160 USD/ton versus last month, bringing PP homo down to 1100 USD/ton/CFR HCMC; PP Film 1126NK offered down to 1190 USD/ton/CFR; PP 1100RC offered down to 1150 USD/ton/CFR; while the PP B1101 extrusion offer fell 180 USD/ton to 1190 USD/ton/CFR.

Although sharply lower than last month, IRPC's offers above are still assessed as high versus the general level. Even though the distributor announced that IRPC's resin plant will undergo maintenance from 19/7 to 15/8, Vietnamese buyers' interest in these goods is also not high.

The strongest price increases early this week came from Chinese-origin PP. One large distributor offered Oriental's PP block copo at 1245-1275 USD/ton/CIF; Jingbo's PP random copo and random pipe around 1300 USD/ton/CIF; and CNPC's and Jinneng China's PP yarn at a high 1260-1265 USD/ton/CIF... These offers are considered far too high versus reality — PP yarn prices are even more than 200 USD/ton above last week's offer level (CNPC's and Jinneng's PP yarn offers to Vietnam last week were 1030-1040 USD/ton/CIF)... so they did not attract buyer interest.

Some suppliers of imported PP to Vietnam this week are temporarily suspending offers to observe further, amid crude oil prices rising above the 80 USD/barrel mark and PP futures on the Dalian exchange (China) rising 50 USD/ton in the first 2 days of the week...

summary: Qamar re-offers up: LLDPE 980-990, HDPE Film F1 1,040 USD/ton CIF
China HDPE Film/yarn raised to 1,150-1,200 CIF; some suspend offers
Domestic LLDPE Film jumped 1,000-1,500 to 30,500-31,000 VND/kg
paras: Today, 15/7, the distributor for Qamar of Saudi Arabia has re-offered PE prices to the Vietnamese market with an upward adjustment versus yesterday morning's offer list. Specifically, Qamar's LLDPE Film grades are offered at 980-990 USD/ton/CIF, and HDPE Film F1 at 1040 USD/ton/CIF. The goods are expected to load in August, with shipping time to the destination port of about 1.5–2 months. The distributor noted that supply is very limited, particularly the F1 grade, which is available in very small quantities.
PE of Chinese origin has also seen suppliers raise offers or temporarily suspend offering. Most HDPE Film/yarn of Chinese origin offered to Vietnam has risen to 1150-1200 USD/ton/CIF.

As for US goods, one distributor quoted offers for Cynpol-origin PE grades to Vietnam trending up 30-40 USD/ton versus last week, owing to higher freight rates. Accordingly, the import offer for Cynpol US LDPE Film grade MI 4 rose to 1140-1150 USD/ton/CIF; LLDPE Film 0218H/0218F is offered at 930 USD/ton/CIF; Cynpol HDPE injection at 950 USD/ton/CIF; and HDPE Film at 980 USD/ton/CIF...

As for Russian goods, compared with last week, the PE offer list from Sibur's distributor at Hai Phong port fluctuated in a tug-of-war this week, with mixed increases and decreases within a range of 100-300 dong/kg, and many HDPE grades reported sold out. Source said Sibur's distributor is prioritizing offers to the Chinese market, where it achieves higher profits.

Accordingly, LLDPE Film at Hai Phong port today is offered at 28,600-28,800 dong/kg incl. VAT, down 200 dong/kg versus last week; the LLDPE film 09200 FE grade rose slightly by 100 dong/kg, and the two LLDPE C6 off-grade items rose 300 dong/kg. Sibur's two HDPE Film/blow off-grade items this week rose slightly by 100 dong/kg versus last week, to 27,700-31,100 dong/kg incl. VAT, while prime HDPE Film and blow were reported sold out...
In domestic trading, many sellers also continued to raise offers for LLDPE and HDPE Film, and even some LDPE items today saw sellers make a mild recovery adjustment.

For example, with LDPE Film, the northern market yesterday still saw some sellers cut offers for Middle East- and Korea-origin LDPE Film to 38,000-39,000 dong/kg incl. VAT, with Lotte Titan LDPE 260GG offered down to 39,000 dong/kg incl. VAT. However today, many trading sellers pushed offers for these items up by about 1,000 dong/kg incl. VAT, bringing the level to 39,000-40,000 dong/kg incl. VAT. Among them, one large trading company offered LDPE 260GG at 40,000 dong/kg incl. VAT; many other trading sellers reported this item sold out, with very limited spot inventory...

As for LLDPE, yesterday, offers for LLDPE Film rose sharply to 30,500-31,000 dong/kg incl. VAT (up about 1,000-1,500 dong/kg from last week's price bottom). Today, 15/7, a leading distributor, Stavian, further raised its offer for Sabic LLDPE 218WJ to 31,200 dong/kg incl. VAT, up slightly by 200 dong/kg from yesterday.

As for HDPE, southern source today said HDPE yarn offers are fluctuating commonly around 35,000-36,000 dong/kg incl. VAT. Spot HDPE yarn inventory is more plentiful than HDPE Film. In fact, among HDPE types, HDPE Film currently sees the most trading sellers raising offers, on the grounds that domestic spot supply is quite limited and some grades are scarce. For example, with Equate Kuwait's HDPE Film 6888, some sellers today offered it at 35,000 dong/kg excl. VAT; that is, the offer price with full 8% VAT reaches 37,800 dong/kg incl. VAT...

summary: Dalian PVC futures jumped nearly 12 USD/ton in today’s session
ACVN issued early August PM66R quote to large customers
Domestic offers mostly flat; last week’s downtrend halted
paras: After the first two days of the week were mostly flat, in today's session, 15/7, PVC futures on the Dalian exchange (China) also jumped nearly 12 USD/ton, mainly supported by sentiment on the general recovery momentum of resin and petrochemical products. In fact, PVC market fundamentals have not changed, with supply increasing while demand is seasonally weak.

In Vietnam, manufacturer AGC Vina (ACVN) has just given a separate quote to some large customers wanting an early price for the August 2026 PVC shipment. Accordingly, ACVN's provisional price for PVC PM66R is 18,800 dong/kg excl. VAT (with full 8% VAT, around 20,300 dong/kg), down sharply by 1,500 dong/kg versus the July shipment offer announced in mid-June. Although this is not yet ACVN's official offer for the August 2026 shipment to the domestic market, the plant's lower quote shows that the price outlook for next month remains weak.

In domestic trading today, PVC offers from domestic distributors were mostly flat. Last week's downtrend was temporarily halted, but there is not yet a clear “reversal” trend as seen in other resin products. The common level for PVC offers and transactions is 20,000-21,500 dong/kg incl. VAT.

PVC is less affected by the US–Iran conflict and the Strait of Hormuz situation because, apart from oil-based PVC supply, the Vietnamese and Chinese markets consume a large volume of coal-based PVC, mainly produced in China.
